title: senecio macroglossoides
intro: Senecio macroglossoides, also known as Groundsel Tree or Butter Print, is a shrub-like plant native to the mountainous regions of South Africa, belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which are attributed to its sesquiterpene lactones and alkaloids. The health benefits of Senecio macroglossoides include its potential to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate respiratory issues. Senecio macroglossoides is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
